response: Implemented in May 2018, the EU’s General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) mandates strict privacy standards for any organization handling EU residents' data, granting individuals rights to access, erase, and port their personal information. Non-compliance carries penalties of up to 4% of global annual revenue or €20 million, leading to major fines including €1.2 billion for Meta, €746 million for Amazon, and over €200 million for Google. Despite criticisms over heavy compliance burdens and uneven enforcement, the GDPR has successfully established data privacy as a fundamental right and inspired similar laws globally, cementing its status as the worldwide benchmark for data protection.
